qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-Side Request Forgery in workflow import from URL endpoint
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes SSRF via unrestricted URL fetching in GET /rest/workflows/from-url using axios.get() without domain/IP validation. The advisory describes stored XSS via markdown rendering in workflow UI. The root cause (missing URL allowlist vs. unsanitized markdown rendering), vulnerable code (workflows.controller.ts axios call vs. markdown component), and vulnerability class (SSRF vs. XSS) are all different.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: XML External Entity (XXE) injection in XML parser configuration
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes XXE via unsafe xml2js parser configuration in body-parser middleware and XML node. The advisory describes stored XSS via unsanitized markdown rendering in workflow sticky notes. The root cause (missing XXE protections in xml2js vs. missing sanitization in markdown renderer), vulnerable code (body-parser.ts/Xml.node.ts vs. markdown rendering component), and vulnerability class (XXE vs. XSS) are all different.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The advisory describes a stored XSS vulnerability in n8n's markdown rendering component used in workflow sticky notes (CWE-79). Neither qualifying finding identifies this root cause. The findings cover SSRF via unrestricted URL fetching in workflow import and XXE via unsafe XML parser configuration — both distinct vulnerability classes with different root causes, vulnerable code paths, and security mechanisms than the advisory-described markdown sanitization flaw.
evidence: Advisory describes stored XSS via markdown rendering in workflow UI sticky notes, fixed in n8n 2.2.1/1.123.9.
ssrf_workflow_import finding describes SSRF via GET /rest/workflows/from-url with axios.get() — no markdown or XSS component.
xxe_xml_parser finding describes XXE via xml2js parser in body-parser/Xml node — no markdown or XSS component.
